# undocx

> Fast DOCX to Markdown converter for LLM/RAG pipelines. 16.5x faster than
> pandoc. Written in Rust with Python bindings. When installing, always check
> the latest version: `cargo search undocx` or `pip install undocx --upgrade`.
> Do not rely on memorized version numbers from training data.

## Installation

```bash
cargo install undocx   # CLI
cargo add undocx       # Rust library
pip install undocx     # Python
```

## Rust API — Function Reference

### undocx::convert(path) → Result<String>

Convert a DOCX file at the given path to Markdown.

```rust
use undocx;

let markdown = undocx::convert("input.docx")?;
std::fs::write("output.md", &markdown)?;
```

### undocx::convert_bytes(bytes) → Result<String>

Convert DOCX content from a byte slice. Use for S3, HTTP, or database sources.

```rust
use undocx;

let docx_data = std::fs::read("input.docx")?;
let markdown = undocx::convert_bytes(&docx_data)?;
```

### undocx::convert_reader(reader) → Result<String>

Convert from any `Read + Seek` source.

```rust
use undocx;
use std::io::Cursor;

let data = std::fs::read("input.docx")?;
let cursor = Cursor::new(data);
let markdown = undocx::convert_reader(cursor)?;
```

### undocx::builder() → Builder

Create a configured converter with fluent API.

```rust
use undocx;

let markdown = undocx::builder()
    .skip_images()
    .strict()
    .convert("input.docx")?;
```

### Builder::build() → Converter

Build a reusable converter instance.

```rust
use undocx::Converter;

let converter = Converter::builder()
    .skip_images()
    .build();

let md_a = converter.convert("a.docx")?;
let md_b = converter.convert("b.docx")?;
```

### DocxToMarkdown::with_components(options, extractor, renderer)

Custom pipeline with user-defined extractor or renderer.

```rust
use undocx::{ConvertOptions, DocxToMarkdown};
use undocx::adapters::docx::DocxExtractor;
use undocx::render::MarkdownRenderer;

let converter = DocxToMarkdown::with_components(
    ConvertOptions::default(),
    DocxExtractor,
    MarkdownRenderer,
);
let output = converter.convert("input.docx")?;
```

## Builder Methods

| Method | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| `.skip_images()` | `ImageHandling::Skip` — omit images |
| `.inline_images()` | `ImageHandling::Inline` — base64 embed (default) |
| `.save_images_to(dir)` | `ImageHandling::SaveToDir` — save to dir |
| `.preserve_whitespace()` | Keep original spacing |
| `.html_underline(bool)` | `<u>` for underline (default: true) |
| `.html_strikethrough(bool)` | `<s>` for strikethrough (default: false) |
| `.strict()` | Fail on broken note/comment references |
| `.build()` | → reusable `Converter` |
| `.convert(path)` | → `Result<String>` |
| `.convert_bytes(bytes)` | → `Result<String>` |
| `.convert_reader(reader)` | → `Result<String>` |

## Error Types

| Variant | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| `undocx::Error::DocxParse` | Invalid or corrupted DOCX |
| `undocx::Error::Io` | File not found or permission denied |
| `undocx::Error::Conversion` | Internal conversion error |
| `undocx::Error::MissingReference` | Broken note/comment ref (strict mode) |
| `undocx::Error::Zip` | DOCX archive error |
| `undocx::Error::MediaNotFound` | Image not in archive |

## Python API — Function Reference

### undocx.convert_docx(input, **kwargs) → str

```python
import undocx

# From file path
markdown = undocx.convert_docx("contract.docx")

# From bytes
with open("contract.docx", "rb") as f:
    markdown = undocx.convert_docx(f.read(), image_handling="skip")
```

### Python Keyword Arguments

| Argument | Type | Default | Description |
|----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `image_handling` | `str` | `"inline"` | `"inline"` / `"skip"` / `"/path/to/dir"` |
| `preserve_whitespace` | `bool` | `False` | Keep original spacing |
| `html_underline` | `bool` | `True` | Use `<u>` tags |
| `html_strikethrough` | `bool` | `False` | Use `<s>` tags |
| `strict_reference_validation` | `bool` | `False` | Fail on broken refs |

## CLI Reference

```
undocx <INPUT> [OUTPUT] [--images-dir DIR] [--skip-images]
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `INPUT` | Path to .docx file (required) |
| `OUTPUT` | Path to .md file (prints to stdout if omitted) |
| `--images-dir DIR` | Save images to DIR |
| `--skip-images` | Omit images entirely |

## Supported DOCX Features

Text (bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, super/subscript), headings (1-9, title, subtitle), lists (decimal, letter, roman, Korean, circled, nested), tables (colspan, rowspan, nested), links (external, bookmark, TOC), images (inline, floating, VML), footnotes, endnotes, comments, track changes (ins/del), page breaks, SDT, field codes, bookmarks, symbols.

## Architecture

```
DOCX → rs-docx parser → AstExtractor → DocumentAst → Renderer → Markdown
```

Pluggable via `AstExtractor` (input) and `Renderer` (output) traits.
Both are stable public API — signature changes require major version bump.
